title: pouteria freitasii
intro: Pouteria freitasii, also known as the Babington's plum or the Guatambu plum, is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to Brazil. Traditionally, Pouteria freitasii has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating gastrointestinal issues. In herbal preparations, Pouteria freitasii is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, although its usage and preparation may vary depending on the region and local traditions.
